label: experimental/frontier
rq: RQ22: separator-failure detector — per-speaker transcript structure for Mode S residual
method: reanalysis only (no Whisper / no ASR run); seven per-speaker-structure features computed from separated_text_per_speaker and the mixed-track metadata. Each feature calibrated two-sidedly at >= 90% specificity on the 40 non-hallucinated tracks. Best detector = lowest permutation p-value (most distinct Mode S profile). Combined = best per-speaker-structure (at 90% spec) OR lang-id entropy (threshold 0.409).
mode_s_definition: hallucinated AND lang_id_entropy < 0.409 AND length_ratio < 2.0 AND cr < 2.4 (escapes every surface detector; the RQ16 corrected-router residual)

mode_s_context.rq19_finding: RQ19 (PR #919) found Mode S is a near-duplicate of the mixed transcript (separator failed, Whisper re-decoded the mixed audio). Content-similarity between sep-concatenated and mix CANNOT discriminate Mode S from clean single-speaker tracks (structural confound). RQ19 limitation #5 suggested per-speaker structure might break the confound.
mode_s_context.mode_s_mechanism: Mode S separated text is concentrated in ONE speaker channel (the other channels are empty or near-empty) and is a near-duplicate of the mixed transcript. Window 22: num_speakers=2 but only 005-F carries text (98 chars, near-duplicate of the 96-char mixed). Window 30: num_speakers=1, 005-F carries 154 chars (near-duplicate of the 150-char mixed).

detectors.note: Shannon entropy over normalised per-speaker separated text lengths
detectors.direction_meaning: flag if score >= threshold (high score = hallucination)

permutation_test.note: Two-sided permutation test: fraction of permutations with |stat| >= |observed|. With n_mode_s=2 the number of distinct labelings is C(77,2)=2926; p-value resolution is bounded and reported with +1 smoothing.

detectors.note: Gini coefficient of per-speaker separated text lengths (incl. empty channels)
detectors.direction_meaning: flag if score >= threshold (high score = hallucination)

best_per_speaker_structure_detector.selection_rule: lowest permutation p-value (most distinct Mode S profile); tiebreak highest Mode S sensitivity at 90% specificity
best_per_speaker_structure_detector.ceiling_interpretation: Max Mode S sensitivity achievable as the specificity floor is relaxed. Reports the deployability gap for per-speaker-structure.

lang_id_entropy_alone_reference.note: RQ13 lang-id entropy detector (no per-speaker-structure). By definition Mode S escapes it (lang-id < 0.409), so lang-id alone misses both Mode S tracks.

combined_detector.note: Combined = best per-speaker-structure detector (at its 90%-specificity operating point) OR lang-id entropy. If per-speaker-structure catches any Mode S track the combined sensitivity rises above lang-id alone (94.6%).

H22a.statement: a per-speaker-structure detector achieves > 50% sensitivity on Mode S tracks (n=2) at > 90% specificity on non-hallucinated tracks (n=40)
H22a.reason: At >= 90% specificity the best per-speaker-structure detector (per_speaker_length_entropy) catches 0% of Mode S. Ceiling analysis reports the max sensitivity achievable as the specificity floor is relaxed.

H22b.statement: combining the best per-speaker-structure detector with lang-id entropy (threshold 0.409 bits) achieves > 95% sensitivity on all 37 hallucinated tracks
H22b.reason: Combined sensitivity is 94.6% (35/37); lang-id alone is 94.6% (35/37). Per-speaker-structure adds 0 hallucinated tracks over lang-id alone (0 of 2 Mode S).

H22c.statement: Mode S tracks have a distinct per-speaker-structure profile (permutation p < 0.05 on >= 1 feature)
H22c.reason: Best feature per_speaker_length_entropy permutation p=0.4508 (4507/10000 extreme). 0 of 7 features have p < 0.05.
